metadata.summary: A Python library for egui-pysync project.

documentation:
# egui-pysync: a server/client for synchronizing states between python and egui UIs

TODO: Write a project documentation

classes.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.__init__
documentation: Initialize the SteteServer.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.states
documentation: Get the state.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.update
documentation:
Update the UI.

Args:
    duration: The duration of the update.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.start
documentation: Start the state server.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.stop
documentation: Stop the state server.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.disconnect_client
documentation: Disconnect actual client.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.is_running
documentation: If state server is running.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.is_connected
documentation: If client is connected to the state server.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.set_error_handler
documentation:
Set the error handler.

Function that will be called when an error occurs in the signals threads. By default, it prints the traceback.
Be careful, if error is not handled, the thread will be stopped.

Args:
    error_handler(Callable[[Exception], None] | None): The error handler function.

methods.name: egui_pysync.server.StateServer.check_workers
documentation: Check all workers threads and restart them if they are stopped.
The main class for the SteteServer for UI.

classes.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueImage

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueImage.set
documentation:
Set the image in the UI image.

Args:
    image(Buffer): The image to set.
    origin(list[int] | tuple[int, int], optional): If set only inner rectangle with given origin (top, left).
                                                   Defaults to None.
    update(bool, optional): Whether to update the UI. Defaults to True.

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueImage.get
documentation:
Get the image in the UI image.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: The image in the UI image. Stape is (height, width, 4). 4 is for RGBA.

classes.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs.get
documentation:
Get the graph by index.

Args:
    idx(int): The index of the graph.

Returns:
    _Graph: The graph object.

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs.set
documentation:
Set the graph to the UI graphs.

If idx is specified and the graph with the index already exists, it will be updated.

Two options for the graph data:
- Data with shape (2, N) where the first row is the x values and the second row is the y values.
- Data with shape (N,) where the x axis is considered to be linear.

Args:
    graph(Buffer): The graph to set. Has to implement the buffer protocol (numpy array).
    idx(int, optional): The index of the graph. If None, smallest available index is used. Defaults to None.
    update(bool, optional): Whether to update the UI. Defaults to False.

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs.remove
documentation:
Remove the graph from the UI graphs.

Args:
    graph(_Graph): The graph object.
    update(bool, optional): Whether to update the UI. Defaults to False.

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs.remove_idx
documentation:
Remove the graph from the UI graphs.

Args:
    idx(int): The index of the graph.
    update(bool, optional): Whether to update the UI. Defaults to False.

methods.name: egui_pysync.structures.ValueGraphs.clear
documentation:
Clear the all UI graphs.

Args:
    update(bool, optional): Whether to update the UI. Defaults to False.
Graph UI element.
